what_it_does: Defines a file-discovered registry for SOURCE-TYPE declarations: small Python modules that declare an origin schema (id, label, join_keys, desc) and are loaded dynamically into a SourceTypeRegistry, validated loudly, and exposed dict-like with a records export.
observations: Discovers source-type modules by scanning directories for non-underscore .py files
Loads each module with importlib.util and expects a module-level SOURCE_TYPE dict
Validates that id exists, is a string, and equals the file name
Validates that join_keys, if present, is a list of strings
SourceType dataclass wraps the raw spec and exposes label/join_keys/desc as properties with fallback label=id
SourceTypeRegistry supports discover, rediscover, register, as_records, and dict-like access
as_records returns the raw specs sorted by id
Ignores files that do not define SOURCE_TYPE
standouts: The join_keys field is explicitly designed for cross-source correlation (e.g., GitHub history joining Claude Code transcripts on author/path/time)
Fail-loud validation on malformed source-type declarations
Dynamic, file-based plugin registration rather than a hardcoded enum
conventions: Follows a registry pattern with dict-like __getitem__, __contains__, __iter__, __len__, get
Uses dataclass for the value object and module-level constants for schema fields
Heavy docstring commentary explaining laws and design rationale
Properties provide defaults rather than raising when a field is absent
concerns: exec_module loads and executes arbitrary Python files from the scanned directories, which is unsafe if directories are untrusted
Duplicate source-type ids are silently overwritten by the last discovered file
rediscover clears the registry before re-scanning, so it is not atomic
No logging or diagnostics for skipped files or load failures
as_records returns raw dicts, not SourceType instances, so consumers lose the property defaults
notes: A small, schema-validated, file-driven plugin registry whose main conceptual hook is join_keys for correlating records across different origins.
questions: Where are the source_types directories configured and populated?
How are join_keys actually consumed when correlating records from different sources?
What is the RelationTypeRegistry this file is said to mirror?
Are there example source_types/*.py files elsewhere in the project?
How does the rest of the board use SourceTypeRegistry at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it registers and validates source-type declarations discovered from files and prepares for cross-source joins.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make 'where a record came from' a first-class, declarative, extensible concept so that new sources (GitHub, transcripts, etc.) can be added by dropping a file and correlated through shared join keys without a data migration.
novelty: The explicit join_keys field and the framing of source types as correlatable origins that can be joined later on shared keys rather than merged by migration.
contribution: A reusable, validated, file-discovered registry for source-type schemas and their correlation keys, enabling the system to add new record origins without code changes to the registry itself.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that dynamically discovers, validates, and exposes source-type declarations from disk as a dict-like registry, supporting cross-source correlation through declared join keys.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a schema-first, extensible registry of record origins so that heterogeneous sources can be registered by file and correlated on shared keys.

symbols.does: Dataclass that wraps a validated source-type declaration.
symbols.description: Holds the source-type id and raw spec, exposing label, join_keys, and desc through properties that supply sensible defaults.

symbols.does: Returns the human label, falling back to the source-type id.
symbols.description: A property that reads the spec's optional label field and returns the id when no label is declared.

symbols.does: Returns the declared correlation keys as a list.
symbols.description: A property that normalizes the optional join_keys field into a list of strings, returning an empty list when none are declared.
A property that exposes the spec's desc field without transformation, returning None if absent.

symbols.does: Dynamically loads a source-type module from a file path.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util to create a module from the file location and executes it, returning the derived module name and the module object.

symbols.does: Validates a raw SOURCE_TYPE dict and builds a SourceType instance.
symbols.description: Performs fail-loud checks for dict shape, required id, id matching file name, known fields, and join_keys type before constructing the value object.
Scans directories for source-type modules, validates and stores them, and provides registration, rediscovery, record export, and dict-like access.
Creates an internal dictionary that will hold validated SourceType objects keyed by id.

symbols.does: Scans directories and registers any valid source-type modules found.
symbols.description: Iterates over the provided directories, skips non-directories, loads each eligible .py file, and registers those that define SOURCE_TYPE.

symbols.does: Clears the registry and re-runs discovery.
symbols.description: Empties the internal source-type map and calls discover again, useful for reloading but not atomic.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a source-type declaration by name.
symbols.description: Builds a SourceType from the given declaration and assigns it into the internal map, overwriting any existing entry with the same name.

symbols.does: Exports all registered source types as sorted raw dicts.
symbols.description: Returns a list of the original spec dicts ordered by source-type id, suitable for serialization or display.
Provides dict-style access that raises KeyError if the id is not present.

symbols.does: Tests whether a given id is registered.
symbols.description: Supports the in operator by checking membership in the internal source-type map.
Returns an iterator over the keys of the internal source-type map.

symbols.does: Returns the count of registered source types.
symbols.description: Supports len() by delegating to the size of the internal source-type map.

symbols.does: Retrieves a SourceType by id with an optional default.
symbols.description: Provides dict-style get access that returns the supplied default if the id is not registered.
